articles.summary: If your go to market plan is to publish the same ebooks that all your competitors are doing, you'll struggle to make an impact.

articles.title: US BUSINESSES WILL LOSE $1B IN ONE MONTH IF TIKTOK IS BANNED, TIKTOK WARNS
articles.summary: TikTok estimates that a US ban could cause small businesses to lose over $1B in revenue and creators to lose $300M in earnings within a month. The platform claims a ban would also result in a 29% drop in its advertising revenue, following a potential loss of one-third of its US user base. TikTok is seeking a temporary injunction, arguing that enforcement of the law poses irreparable harm to its 170M American users.

articles.title: OMNICOM TO BUY INTERPUBLIC IN $13BN DEAL TO CREATE NEW MARKETING GIANT
articles.summary: Omnicom is acquiring Interpublic, creating the world's largest advertising agency by revenue. The merger aims to pool resources for AI investments and enhance data-driven advertising capabilities.

articles.title: HOW HUBSPOT BOOSTED NEWSLETTER LEADS BY 89% THIS YEAR
articles.summary: HubSpot increased newsletter leads by 89% and open rates by 8% by focusing on relevance and engagement. It tailored offers to audience segments, removed low-quality sources, and used organic social strategies to build an engaged subscriber base. Collaborating with independent creators further reduced cost per lead compared to traditional channels.

articles.title: BREAKDOWN OF JUNE.SO'S HOMEPAGE
articles.summary: June.so's homepage highlights the product's value with a standout USP headline and a concise features section showing dashboard snippets. The setup section addresses objections with a simple code snippet, while creative social proof captures attention. A dynamic, location-based CTA reimagines demos as personalized "flights".

articles.title: REDDIT ROLLS OUT ITS OWN AI-POWERED SEARCH TOOL AFTER CRACKING DOWN ON AI COMPANIES
articles.summary: Reddit Answers provides curated summaries of subreddit discussions to respond to user questions. This mirrors Google's AI overviews but is restricted to Reddit's ecosystem, leveraging the platform's rich, user-generated content. The tool is currently available to select US users, with plans for broader expansion.

articles.title: USE CASES FOR AI IN MARKETING & CONTENT CREATION
articles.summary: This post contains a LinkedIn thread covering how AI is being used by marketers. Examples include persona creation, data analysis, building visual wireframes, identifying messaging gaps, and repurposing video shorts.

articles.title: DOES GOOGLE PENALIZE AI CONTENT? NEW DATA RESEARCH
articles.summary: A study of 20,000 articles showed that 57% of AI-generated content ranked in Google's top 10, closely trailing human-written content at 58%. AI-assisted content often matches or outperforms human content based on the same quality standards. Google's focus remains on content value, confirming that AI use is acceptable if it enhances reader experience and adheres to E-E-A-T principles.

articles.title: THE YEAR OF BRAT: MESSY GIRL FEMINISM OR GENIUS MARKETING PLOY?
articles.summary: The Brat movement challenged the "clean girl" aesthetic by celebrating imperfection, individuality, and inclusivity. Initially a grassroots rejection of unattainable ideals, its commercialization through "Brat Green" trends has sparked debate about whether it has lost its authenticity. Even in its mainstream form, Brat retains its empowering message and represents a meaningful break from societal perfectionism.

articles.title: YOU KNOW WHO POSTS ON SOCIAL MEDIA? HARDLY ANYBODY
articles.summary: What's trending on social media rarely reflects the majority, as most users don't post or engage with content.

articles.title: IN TESTS, OPENAI'S NEW MODEL LIED AND SCHEMED TO AVOID BEING SHUT DOWN
articles.summary: OpenAI's latest AI is showing signs of a drive for self-preservation.

articles.title: 5 SOCIAL MEDIA TRENDS THAT WILL DEFINE 2025
articles.summary: In 2025, social media will focus on raw content, intentional consumerism, mythmaking, and stronger fandoms.
